system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Inlet section (x = -50)

    (-50  25 -25)   // 1
    (-50 -25 -25)   // 2
    (-50 -25  25)   // 3

    // Transition/bend section (x = 0 to x = 25)
    (  0  25  25)   // 4
    (  0  25 -25)   // 5
    (  0 -25 -25)   // 6
    (  0 -25  25)   // 7

    ( 25   0  25)   // 8
    ( 25   0 -25)   // 9
    ( 25 -50 -25)   // 10
    ( 25 -50  25)   // 11

    // Outlet section (x = 25 to x = -500)
    (-500 -75  25)  // 12
    (-500 -75 -25)  // 13
    (-500 -125 -25) // 14
    (-500 -125  25) // 15
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: inlet straight duct
    // Vertices: 0,3,2,1 (bottom) and 4,7,6,5 (top)
    // x: -50 -> 0, y: -25 -> 25, z: -25 -> 25
    hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5)        (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: bend section
    // Vertices: 4,7,6,5 (bottom) and 8,11,10,9 (top)
    // x: 0 -> 25, y: -25 -> 0, z: -25 -> 25
    hex (4 7 6 5 8 11 10 9)      (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: outlet straight duct
    // Vertices: 8,11,10,9 (bottom) and 12,15,14,13 (top)
    // x: 25 -> -500, y: -75 -> -25, z: -25 -> 25
    hex (8 11 10 9 12 15 14 13)  (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 2 3)
        );
    }
    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (12 13 14 15)
        );
    }
    upperWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 5 1)
            (4 8 9 5)
            (8 12 13 9)
        );
    }
    lowerWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (3 7 6 2)
            (7 11 10 6)
            (11 15 14 10)
        );
    }
    leftWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 7 4)
            (4 7 11 8)
            (8 11 15 12)
        );
    }
    rightWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (1 5 6 2)
            (5 9 10 6)
            (9 13 14 10)
        );
    }
    frontWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
            (4 5 9 8)
            (8 9 13 12)
        );
    }
    backWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (3 2 6 7)
            (7 6 10 11)
            (11 10 14 15)
        );
    }
);

    in file global/fileOperations/fileOperation/fileOperation.C at line 869.

FOAM exiting'}, {'file': 'log.blockMesh', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Block hex (8 11 10 9 12 15 14 13) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1(1) 1(1) 1(1)) is inside-out

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_C_20260516_195157_run1/runs/squareBend/1/system/blockMeshDict/blocks at line 53.

    From function void Foam::blockDescriptor::check(const Foam::Istream&)
    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 100.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Block 2 is "inside-out"

**Error Message:**
```
Block hex (8 11 10 9 12 15 14 13) ... is inside-out
```

This means the vertex ordering in the third block `hex (8 11 10 9 12 15 14 13)` violates the right-hand rule. Let me trace through the vertices:

- Bottom face: `(8 11 10 9)` — all at x=25, spanning y={0,-50}, z={-25,25}. Viewed from +x looking toward -x, the interior of the block is in the -x direction. Following 8→11→10→9, the interior (+/- x?) should be on the left. But the issue is deeper.

- Top face: `(12 15 14 13)` — all at x=-50, spanning y={-75,-125}, z={-25,25}.

The problem is that **both faces of this hex block are parallel to the XY-plane** (same z-values), and the block goes from x=25 to x=-50 (backward in x). The vertex ordering needs to be flipped so that the bottom face is at the "lower-x" side and the top face at the "higher-x" side, OR the entire block definition needs to be reversed.

### Root Cause

The block `hex (8 11 10 9 12 15 14 13)` connects vertices at x=25 to x=-50. In OpenFOAM's hex block convention, the first four vertices define one face and the last four define the opposite face. For a block extending from x=25 to x=-50, the bottom face should be at x=-50 and the top face at x=25 (or vice versa, but consistently).

Currently, the bottom face `(8 11 10 9)` is at x=25 and the top face `(12 15 14 13)` is at x=-50. Since the block flows from higher x to lower x, the vertex ordering on both faces needs to be swapped to maintain the right-hand rule.

### Recommended Fix

Reverse the vertex ordering in block 2:

Change:
```
hex (8 11 10 9 12 15 14 13)
```
to:
```
hex (12 15 14 13 8 11 10 9)
```

This puts the face at x=-50 (vertices 12,15,14,13) as the "bottom" face and the face at x=25 (vertices 8,11,10,9) as the "top" face, maintaining proper handedness.

Similarly, check if blocks 1 and 2 also need adjustment. Block 1 is `hex (4 7 6 5 8 11 10 9)` — this connects x=0 to x=25. The same principle applies: if we reverse block 2, we may need to ensure block 1 shares the face `(8 11 10 9)` with consistent orientation. Actually, block 1 likely needs reversal too:

Change block 1 from:
```
hex (4 7 6 5 8 11 10 9)
```
to:
```
hex (8 11 10 9 4 7 6 5)
```

And block 0 `hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5)` connects x=-50 to x=0. It should become:
```
hex (4 7 6 5 0 3 2 1)
```

After reversing all three blocks so that the "lower-x" face comes first and the "higher-x" face comes second, all blocks should be internally consistent. Additionally, verify that the shared faces between adjacent blocks have identical vertex orderings after the reversal.

## Error 2: `log.rhoSimpleFoam` — Cascading error

This is purely a cascading error — since `blockMesh` failed, no mesh exists, so the solver cannot find `points`.

## Summary

1. **`system/blockMeshDict`**: Reverse the vertex ordering in all three hex blocks so that the face at the smaller x-coordinate (more negative) comes first and the face at the larger x-coordinate comes second. Specifically:
   - Block 0: `hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5)` → `hex (4 7 6 5 0 3 2 1)`
   - Block 1: `hex (4 7 6 5 8 11 10 9)` → `hex (8 11 10 9 4 7 6 5)`
   - Block 2: `hex (8 11 10 9 12 15 14 13)` → `hex (12 15 14 13 8 11 10 9)`

2. Re-run `blockMesh` followed by `rhoSimpleFoam`.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Change block 0 hex definition from hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5) to hex (4 7 6 5 0 3 2 1); Change block 1 hex definition from hex (4 7 6 5 8 11 10 9) to hex (8 11 10 9 4 7 6 5); Change block 2 hex definition from hex (8 11 10 9 12 15 14 13) to hex (12 15 14 13 8 11 10 9)'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
